channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to turn an AI prototype into a production system
item.description: AI tools have changed how fast software gets off the ground. Today, a single developer can go from an idea to a working AI prototype in days, sometimes hours. In a controlled study on GitHub...

item.title: How Data Lake Table Storage Degrades Over Time
item.description: This is Part 9 of a 15-part Apache Iceberg Masterclass. Part 8 covered embedded catalogs. This article explains the five ways Iceberg table storage degrades and how to detect each problem before...

item.title: Partition and Sort Keys on DynamoDB: Modeling data for batch-and-stream convergence
item.description: How to design partition keys, sort keys and a serving layer when your data has two sources with different SLAs Batch pipelines are reliable, auditable, and built to handle complexity at scale, but...

item.title: Text Degeneration: A Production Failure Mode That Most Benchmarks Do Not Track
Auto-Generate Optimized GitHub Actions Workflows For Any Stack With This New CLI Tool
item.description: We’ve all been there. You finish building a new project. It’s clean, it’s working, and it’s ready to ship. But then comes the worst part: Writing the CI/CD pipeline. You open Google, copy a...

item.title: Unchaining the African Creator Economy
item.description: The Problem We Were Actually Solving We were tasked with creating a platform that would allow creators to sell digital products directly to their audience, bypassing traditional payment gateways...

item.title: The Treasure Hunt Engine Gotcha - A Lesson in Constrained Performance
item.description: The Problem We Were Actually Solving When we first set out to optimize the Treasure Hunt Engine, our primary goal was to improve player engagement. We wanted to make the treasure hunt experience...

item.title: great_cto v2.17 - no more tambourine dance
item.description: If you've ever spent 20 minutes setting up Claude Code plugins before you could even start working - this update is for you. One install, everything works. Previously: install great_cto, then...

item.title: When Catalogs Are Embedded in Storage
item.description: This is Part 8 of a 15-part Apache Iceberg Masterclass. Part 7 covered the traditional catalog landscape. This article examines a newer approach: embedding the catalog directly inside the storage...

item.title: SafeMind AI: Instant Health & Safety Intelligence
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4 What I Built The Problem It Solves: During health or safety emergencies, people often face panic, confusion, and language...

item.title: Even If You Hate AI, You Will Use Google AI Search
item.description: The search giant’s AI-crafted answers are so convenient, you’ll be sucked in—to the detriment of the web and the artists and thinkers behind it.

item.title: The Trump phone is not here
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. It's now been more than a week since Trump Mobile...

item.title: The literary world isn’t prepared for AI
item.description: Since 2012, the British literary magazine Granta has published the regional winners of the annual Commonwealth Short Story Prize. This year, however, there was something off about one of the...

item.title: Why would you disrespect your favorite artist with an AI remix?
item.description: AI covers and remixes of songs are already a blight on the internet. Spotify, YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram are awash in flat reggae versions of "Smells Like Teen Spirit," dinky country...

item.title: Tesla recalls thousands of Model Ys at risk of… missing a sticker
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has issued a recall for 14,575 Model Y vehicles over an issue that Tesla can't fix with its usual software update: a missing label....

item.title: Boots Riley turns class struggle into comedy with I Love Boosters
item.description: Before Boots Riley became the writer / director / musician behind Sorry to Bother You and I'm a Virgo, he was a young community organizer fighting for social justice as part of the Progressive...

item.title: CISA Security Leak
item.description: Crazy story: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly...

item.title: The post-search Google era begins
item.description: Google is many things, but most of the time it's a verb: It is what it is to search the internet for information. But what if it's an AI agent doing the searching? And it's doing it proactively?...

item.title: The Boys limped through its last season, but made up for it with the finale
item.description: It's hard existing in a world seemingly designed for the undeservingly powerful and painfully boring few. They have been gifted what many of us only dream of: the ability to effectively do...

item.title: If I could only have one laptop for work and gaming, I’d get this one
item.description: I've loved the Asus ROG Zephyrus G14 since it launched in 2020. I bought one for my wife after reading about how awesome it was on a little site called The Verge dot com. Since then, the G14 has...

item.title: The Download: coding’s future, the ‘Steroid Olympics,’ and AI-driven science
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s...

item.title: Megalodon GitHub Attack Targets 5,561 Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new automated campaign called Megalodon that has pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window. "Using...

item.title: Making Vulnerable Drivers Exploitable Without Hardware - The BYOVD Perspective
item.description: 1 Introduction This article provides a technical analysis of how many Windows kernel mode drivers can be interacted with from user mode without the hardware they were developed for. This work was...

item.title: Samsung’s memory chip employees negotiated $340,000 bonuses this year
item.description: Details have emerged about a tentative deal struck between Samsung and semiconductor employees who had threatened to strike. The deal reportedly makes some workers eligible for average annual...

item.title: Microsoft’s consumer marketing chief to leave next year
item.description: Yusuf Mehdi, executive vice president and consumer chief marketing officer at Microsoft, is leaving the company after 35 years. Mehdi announced his departure in an internal memo on Thursday,...

item.title: Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
item.description: During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the...

item.title: The Enhanced Games fit right in with the rest of 2026’s longevity vibes
item.description: This Sunday, a group of 42 athletes will gather in Las Vegas to compete in a somewhat unusual sporting competition. Participants in the inaugural Enhanced Games are being encouraged to take...

item.title: The Gulf’s AI Boom Has an Undersea Cable Problem
item.description: Hyperscalers are pushing the Gulf to rethink internet infrastructure as AI raises the stakes of cable disruptions.

item.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf. In...
